responsibilities: Design and implement real-time SLAM pipelines that fuse camera, LiDAR, and IMU data for accurate robot localization and mapping
Develop visual-inertial odometry algorithms to estimate robot pose with low drift in GPS-denied and indoor environments
Implement loop closure detection and pose graph optimization to correct accumulated drift in long-duration mapping sessions
Build and tune Kalman filter and particle filter based state estimation systems for robust multi-sensor localization
Optimize SLAM algorithms using bundle adjustment and graph-based optimization techniques to improve map accuracy and consistency
Profile and optimize SLAM software to run in real time within compute and power constraints on embedded robot hardware
Evaluate SLAM system accuracy against ground-truth trajectories using standard metrics such as absolute trajectory error
Collaborate with perception and planning teams to ensure localization outputs meet downstream navigation accuracy requirements
Test and validate SLAM performance across diverse environments including indoor, outdoor, and GPS-denied scenarios
